findings.description: humanize_delta caps its largest unit at weeks, so anything older than ~7 weeks renders as absurd values like '300 w ago' instead of months/years, violating the 'coarse human description' contract.
parse_iso_date claims to parse 'YYYY-MM-DD strictly' but strptime accepts non-zero-padded and short forms like '2024-1-1' or '1-2-3', so 'strictly' is a lie the format string cannot back up.

day_range eagerly materializes one date object per day, so wide ranges (e.g. multi-millennium) allocate millions of objects, and business_days_between builds that whole list just to sum a generator over it.
clamp_to_range validates bound ordering but not tz-awareness, so a naive value against aware bounds raises a raw TypeError from min/max instead of the module's consistent ValueError contract.
start_of_day is typed for date but silently accepts a datetime (a date subclass) and discards its time/tzinfo, a quiet truncation footgun.
is_weekend is the one public function with no docstring while every sibling has one, an inconsistency a linter would flag.
